design_philosophy.core_loop: Progress through horror-themed stages, defeat grotesque bosses using melee combat and scattered weapons, advance narrative of Rick rescuing Jennifer from the Terror Mask's influence
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Guilt and redemption—Rick is haunted by his failure to save Jennifer in the first game; the mask tempts him with a second chance, creating internal conflict between desire for power and moral uncertainty
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Exacting violent revenge on childhood fears and monsters; embodying a masked vigilante with superhuman strength granted by a supernatural artifact
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unapologetic embrace of gore and shock value as core aesthetic. The game leans heavily into horror imagery and blood as gameplay identity rather than consequence. Narrative delivered through mask manipulation and temptation, positioning the player as potentially corrupted rather than heroic.

difficulty.continues_system: Password system in English version allows resuming from checkpoints; Japanese version lacks this feature
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing via password system (English/NA versions)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Selectable difficulty setting available, a new addition to the series that gives players control over challenge level

progression.upgrade_system: Weapons scattered throughout levels provide temporary combat enhancement; no persistent upgrade tree
progression.unlock_mechanism: Eight stages unlock sequentially; stage completion gates access to subsequent content
replayability_hooks: Multiple difficulty settings encourage replay at higher challenge
Password system enables non-linear stage access for veterans
Gore and horror imagery serves as primary replay motivation for genre fans

world_building_techniques: Opening narration establishes three-month time gap and Rick's guilt/nightmares
Terror Mask serves as recurring antagonist voice, delivering exposition and temptation through direct address to player
Stage progression mirrors descent into supernatural realm (from charred ruins to hidden mansion to land of dead)
Regional localization differences affect clarity: Japanese version explicitly states Jennifer is in 'land of the dead' while Western version leaves it ambiguous

boss_design.philosophy: Each stage culminates in encounter with grotesque monster boss; bosses function as thematic horror encounters that reinforce the game's gore-focused identity

faction_indicators: Terror Mask cult/influence (temptation toward power at moral cost)
Victims/innocents (Jennifer as catalyst, implied other trapped souls in land of dead)
danger_signals: Rick's willingness to accept mask's power despite knowing its corruption
Nightmares and guilt-driven desperation making him vulnerable to manipulation
The mask's ability to reform and reassert control after apparent destruction
environmental_storytelling: Charred ruins of West Mansion establish prior tragedy and Rick's failure
Progression from ruins to intact hidden mansion to supernatural realm visualizes descent
Grotesque monster encounters illustrate the horrific consequences of mask's influence
